title: 360° Push mid-band: feather wedge seam + model panorama Gaussian-blur rig
goal: 360° Push landed at 21.85 dB (T-q360zoom01) via a hard centre-anchored B-reveal wedge over a −86px/frame panning A + static-home B. The mid-band f8–f18 sits at ~14–16 dB. Two unmodelled effects remain: (1) the A/B seam in the GT is SOFT/feathered, not the hard step the current wedge draws — add a few-px alpha ramp at the wedge edge and re-fit the edge position; (2) the .motr equirect group has a 'Gaussian Blur' filter (360° Aware=1) whose Rig-Behavior Amount curve ramps 0→100 across the transition — the panoramas are progressively blurred mid-transition (GT lapvar 79→52), which the model does not apply. Apply the decoded blur ramp to both A and B (or to the incoming/outgoing card) and re-measure per-frame. Target: lift f8–f18 above 16 dB without regressing the perfect endpoints (f00/f23 ≈ 41 dB). Cite the .motr Rig-Behavior Amount curve and the wedge-edge feather width in a comment.
